channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US announces India’s planned $500 billion goods purchase over five years amid trade talks
item.description: The United States Secretary of State Marco Rubio has stated that India has committed to purchasing around $500 billion worth of American goods over the next five years. He made this announcement after...

item.title: Reopened Train des Merveilles railway connects Nice with the Southern French Alps
item.description: The article describes the reopening of the Train des Merveilles (Train of Wonders), a scenic railway line in southeastern France that links the glamorous Côte d’Azur with the rugged landscapes of the ...

item.title: Study highlights Shock Index tool for early detection of life-threatening childbirth complications
item.description: The article explains the major global challenge of maternal deaths caused by postpartum haemorrhage and maternal sepsis, conditions that remain especially severe in low- and middle-income countries, i...

item.title: Twelve writers selected for first Bangsamoro Writers Workshop in Cotabato City
item.description: Twelve writers have been officially selected as fellows for the inaugural Bangsamoro Writers Workshop, which is set to take place from June 4 to June 7 in Cotabato City. The initiative is organized by...

item.title: Penitani scores two tries in impressive NRL debut for the Rabbitohs
item.description: Talanoa Penitani made a memorable start to his NRL career by scoring two tries on debut for the Rabbitohs. In a standout performance highlighted in match footage, Penitani crossed the line twice, imme...

item.title: Victorian Premier denies evidence of bikie involvement in North East Link project amid corruption concerns
item.description: Victoria’s Premier Jacinta Allan has stated there is currently no evidence that outlaw motorcycle gangs, commonly referred to as bikies, have financially benefited from the North East Link infrastruct...

item.title: US and Iran advance towards draft peace agreement to end West Asia conflict; Pakistan proposes hosting negotiations
item.description: The United States and Iran have moved closer to finalising a potential agreement aimed at formally ending the ongoing West Asia conflict, with U.S. President Donald Trump stating that a deal has been ...

item.title: Conference of the Left seeks coordination of South Africa’s working class ahead of local elections
item.description: South Africa continues to grapple with deep structural economic and social challenges, including persistently high unemployment, widening inequality, and an economic system still heavily influenced by...

item.title: Stephen Colbert appears on Michigan public access programme with Jack White following The Late Show finale
item.description: Stephen Colbert made an unexpected return to television just one day after the final episode of The Late Show With Stephen Colbert aired on CBS. The late-night programme concluded on 21 May 2026 after...

item.title: Former Reps Member Zakari Mohammed Wins ADC Governorship Ticket in Kwara
item.description: Former member of the House of Representatives, Zakari Mohammed, don emerge as the governorship candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) for the 2027 governorship election for Kwara State. Za...

item.title: Tanzania Studies China’s SASAC Framework to Reform State-Owned Enterprises
item.description: Tanzania inaangalia uwezekano wa kutumia mfumo wa China wa State-owned Assets Supervision and Administration Commission (SASAC) kama sehemu ya mageuzi ya kuboresha usimamizi na ufanisi wa mashirika ya...

item.title: Rivers Police and Stakeholders Urge Students to Avoid Cultism and Drug Abuse in Port Harcourt Sensitisation Programme
item.description: The Divisional Crime Officer (DCO) of Rumuodomaya Police Station, CSP Dindara Dadamu, has issued a strong warning to students in Rivers State to stay away from cultism and drug abuse, stressing that s...

item.title: Rescue Teams Search for Missing Workers After Building Collapse in Angeles City
item.description: Authorities in the Philippines launched a major rescue operation after a nine-storey building under construction collapsed in Angeles City near Manila on Sunday, May 24. According to local officials, ...

item.title: Public social media dispute erupts between Baba Levo and Abdul Nondo
item.description: Tanzanian entertainment personalities Baba Levo and Abdul Nondo have found themselves at the centre of a heated public disagreement that has unfolded mainly through social media platforms. The dispute...

item.title: Fuel price increases drive up transport and living costs across South Africa
item.description: South Africans are facing growing financial pressure as recent sharp fuel price increases ripple through transport, food, and household budgets. Petrol rose by R3.27 per litre and diesel by more than ...

item.title: Vera-Ellen, Hollywood Musical Dancer and MGM Star of the 1940s–1950s
item.description: Vera-Ellen (1921–1981) was an American actress, singer, and dancer who became one of the standout performers of Hollywood’s Golden Age of musical films. She was widely admired for her extraordinary te...

item.title: Shuttle train blast in Quetta leaves several dead; investigation underway
item.description: A deadly explosion struck a shuttle train near Chaman Phatak in Quetta on Sunday morning, resulting in multiple fatalities and injuries, according to officials and state-run media reports. The shuttle...

item.title: Joshua Garcia says he is unsure if he has ever been cheated on
item.description: Actor Joshua Garcia opened up in a recent interview about his thoughts on past relationships and the possibility of having been cheated on, saying he is not sure if it ever happened. Speaking in a can...

item.title: Bomb attack near Quetta derails passenger train and injures dozens in Balochistan
item.description: A powerful explosion near a railway track in the southwestern Pakistani city of Quetta has injured more than two dozen people after it struck a passenger train travelling through the area. According t...

item.title: Death toll in Shanxi coal mine gas explosion revised to 82 as China orders investigation
item.description: Chinese authorities have revised the death toll in the Shanxi coal mine disaster to 82 after initial reports stated that at least 90 people had died in the gas explosion. The accident took place late ...
